response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a strong full-stack and distributed-systems engineer with solid TypeScript/Node.js depth and good software-craftsmanship practices, but the role's core requirement — senior C++ expertise — is almost entirely absent from her profile. C++ appears only in a 6-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis project; it is not even listed in her skills section. The role further requires industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), SDL practices, multi-threaded systems design, and IoT protocol knowledge (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), none of which surface in her history. Her adjacent strengths (event-driven architecture, REST, Node.js) are listed as advantageous extras, not substitutes for the hard C++ and industrial-domain requirements.
strengths: Broad distributed-systems and microservices background with demonstrated high-throughput, low-latency work (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby — millions of HTTP requests/sec)
Consistent agile craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing — aligns with the collaborative, quality-minded behavioral profile
Node.js and React exposure maps to the advantageous-skills list, and event-driven GISCollective platform shows architectural thinking
concerns: C++ proficiency is negligible — only 6 months at National Instruments and a thesis project; it is absent from the skills section entirely
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity, SDL, IEC 62443, or any of the required IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
Career trajectory is firmly web/TypeScript/Node.js; the 8–12 year experience window is in a completely different domain than what the role demands

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and event-driven architecture experience
